title: androsace occidentalis subsp. acuta
intro: Androsace occidentalis subsp. acuta is a subspecies of the Western Rock Jasmine, a flowering plant native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a potential remedy for skin conditions and infections. Androsace occidentalis subsp. acuta can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
